mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market deterioration signals impending recessionary pressures that compress corporate earnings and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses 4.5%, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and consumer price indices indicate robust consumer demand pricing power, which expands corporate profit margins and produces positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in greater than 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output that boosts short-term market sentiment and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during recovery phases, producing negative 5-day returns in greater than 50% of observations, or IC falls below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product establishes a baseline of economic expansion that supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in greater than 55% of quarters following positive GDPC1 revisions, or rolling IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 260, accelerating inflation triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive in greater than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 260, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and generates positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative returns in greater than 50% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month declines in housing starts signal weakening construction demand and broader economic slowdown, which reduces forward earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Housing start declines fail to predict negative returns, with positive forward returns occurring in greater than 55% of triggered months, or signal IC drops below -0.02.
